info.title: CLI Version:
info.description: A CLI for interacting with the Beamable Cloud.

options.name: --dryrun
options.description:
[DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible
without making any network calls

options.name: --cid
--engine
options.description:
If passed, sets the engine integration that is calling
for the command

options.name: --engine-sdk-version
options.description:
The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that
Engine

options.name: --engine-version
options.description: The version of the engine that is calling the CLI

options.name: --pid
options.description:
PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any
Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in
'.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
When true, skip input waiting and use default
arguments (or error if no defaults are possible)
[default: False]

options.name: --host
options.description:
This option defines the target Beamable environment.
Needed for private cloud customers to target their
exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone
else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --access-token
options.description:
The access token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json
for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --refresh-token
options.description:
A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites
the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS
INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a
./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool,
then any global invocation of the beam tool will
automatically redirect and call the local version.
However, there will be a performance penalty due to
the extra process invocation. This option flag will
cause an error to occur instead of automatically
redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally
running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to
use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be
visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will
attempt to resolve docker through its usual install
locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE
environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

options.name: --emit-log-streams
options.description:
Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to
however they are logged

options.name: --add-project-path
options.description:
additional file paths to be included when building a
local project manifest.

options.name: --raw
options.description:
Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by
default when the command is being piped

options.name: --pretty
options.description:
Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by
default when the command is not piped

options.name: --dotnet-path
options.description: a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --help
options.description:
Show help and usage information
Raw Output:
Returns a stream of AccountMeCommandOutput object on the stream stream, which each may resemble the following...
{
"ts": 1774693724241,
"type": "stream",
"data": {
"id": 0,
"email": "user@example.com",
"language": "en",
"scopes": [],
"thirdPartyAppAssociations": [],
"deviceIds": [],
"external": [],
"roleString": null,
"roles": null,
"tokenCid": null,
"tokenPid": null,
"accessToken": null,
"refreshToken": null,
"tokenExpiration": "0001-01-01T00:00:00",
"tokenIssuedAt": "0001-01-01T00:00:00",
"tokenExpiresIn": 0
}
}
Returns a single message of NoTokenError object on the errorNoTokenError stream, which each may resemble the following...
{
"ts": 1774693724311,
"type": "errorNoTokenError",
"data": {
"message": null,
"invocation": null,
"exitCode": 0,
"typeName": null,
"fullTypeName": null,
"stackTrace": null
}
}
Returns a single message of InvalidTokenError object on the errorInvalidTokenError stream, which each may resemble the following...
{
"ts": 1774693724313,
"type": "errorInvalidTokenError",
"data": {
"serverError": null,
"message": null,
"invocation": null,
"exitCode": 0,
"typeName": null,
"fullTypeName": null,
"stackTrace": null
}
}

commands.name: config
commands.description: List the current beamable configuration

options.name: --no-overrides
options.description:
Whether this command should ignore the local config
overrides [default: False]

options.name: --set
options.description:
When true, whatever '--host', '--cid', '--pid' values
you provide will be set. If '--no-overrides' is true,
this will set the version controlled configuration
file. If not, this will set the local overrides file
inside the .beamable/temp directory [default: False]

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
When true, skip input waiting and use default
arguments (or error if no defaults are possible)
[default: False]

options.name: --host
options.description:
This option defines the target Beamable environment.
Needed for private cloud customers to target their
exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone
else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --access-token
options.description:
The access token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json
for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --refresh-token
options.description:
A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites
the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS
INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a
./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool,
then any global invocation of the beam tool will
automatically redirect and call the local version.
However, there will be a performance penalty due to
the extra process invocation. This option flag will
cause an error to occur instead of automatically
redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally
running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to
use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be
visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will
attempt to resolve docker through its usual install
locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE
environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

however they are logged

local project manifest.

options.name: --host
options.description:
This option defines the target Beamable environment.
Needed for private cloud customers to target their
exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone
else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --access-token
options.description:
The access token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json
for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --refresh-token
options.description:
A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites
the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS
INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a
./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool,
then any global invocation of the beam tool will
automatically redirect and call the local version.
However, there will be a performance penalty due to
the extra process invocation. This option flag will
cause an error to occur instead of automatically
redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally
running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to
use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be
visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will
attempt to resolve docker through its usual install
locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE
environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

options.description:
Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to
however they are logged

options.description:
additional file paths to be included when building a
local project manifest.

options.name: --filter-type
options.description:
Defines the semantics for the `filter`
<ExactIds|Regexes|Tags|TypeHierarchy|TypeHierarc  argument. When no filters are given, affects
hyStrict>                                         all existing content.
ExactIds => Will only add the given tags to the
','-separated list of filters
Regexes => Will add the given tags to any
content whose Id is matched by any of the
','-separated list of filters (C# regex string)
TypeHierarchy => Will add the given tags to any
content of the ','-separated list of filters
(content type strings with full hierarchy ---
StartsWith comparison)
Tags => Will add the given tags to any content
that currently has any of the ','-separated
list of filters (tags) [default: ExactIds]

options.name: --filter
options.description:
Accepts different strings to filter which
content files will be affected. See the
`filter-type` option []

options.name: --auto-snapshot-type
options.description:
Defines if after publish the Content System should
<Both|LocalOnly|None|SharedOnly>             take snapshots of the content.
None => Will not save any snapshot after publishing
LocalOnly => Will save the snapshot under
`.beamable/temp/content-snapshots/[PID]` folder
SharedOnly => Will save the snapshot under
`.beamable/content-snapshots/[PID]` folder
Both => Will save two snapshots, under local and
shared folders [default: None]

options.name: --max-local-snapshots
options.description:
Defines the max stored local snapshots taken by the
auto snapshot generation by this command. When the
number hits, the older one will be deletd and
replaced by the new snapshot [default: 20]

commands.name: sync
commands.description: Synchronizes the local content matching the filters to the latest content stored in the realm

options.name: --manifest-ids
options.description:
Inform a subset of ','-separated manifest ids
for which to return data. By default, will
return just the global manifest [default:
global]

options.name: --filter-type
options.description:
Defines the semantics for the `filter`
<ExactIds|Regexes|Tags|TypeHierarchy|TypeHierarc  argument. When no filters are given, affects
hyStrict>                                         all existing content.
ExactIds => Will only add the given tags to the
','-separated list of filters
Regexes => Will add the given tags to any
content whose Id is matched by any of the
','-separated list of filters (C# regex string)
TypeHierarchy => Will add the given tags to any
content of the ','-separated list of filters
(content type strings with full hierarchy ---
StartsWith comparison)
Tags => Will add the given tags to any content
that currently has any of the ','-separated
list of filters (tags) [default: ExactIds]

options.name: --filter
options.description:
Accepts different strings to filter which
content files will be affected. See the
`filter-type` option []

options.name: --sync-created
options.description:
Deletes any created content that is not present
in the latest manifest. If filters are
provided, will only delete the created content
that matches the filter

options.name: --sync-modified
options.description:
This will discard your local changes ONLY on
files that are NOT conflicted. If filters are
provided, will only do this for content that
matches the filter

options.name: --sync-conflicts
options.description:
This will discard your local changes ONLY on
files that ARE conflicted. If filters are
provided, will only do this for content that
matches the filter

options.name: --sync-deleted
options.description:
This will revert all your deleted files. If
filters are provided, will only do this for
content that matches the filter

options.name: --target
options.description:
If you pass in a Manifest's UID, we'll sync
with that as the target. If filters are
provided, will only do this for content that
matches the filter

options.description:
Defines the semantics for the `filter`
<ExactIds|Regexes|Tags|TypeHierarchy|TypeHierarc  argument. When no filters are given, affects
hyStrict>                                         all existing content.
ExactIds => Will only add the given tags to the
','-separated list of filters
Regexes => Will add the given tags to any
content whose Id is matched by any of the
','-separated list of filters (C# regex string)
TypeHierarchy => Will add the given tags to any
content of the ','-separated list of filters
(content type strings with full hierarchy ---
StartsWith comparison)
Tags => Will add the given tags to any content
that currently has any of the ','-separated
list of filters (tags) [default: ExactIds]

options.name: --pid
options.description:
PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any
Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in
'.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
When true, skip input waiting and use default
arguments (or error if no defaults are possible)
[default: False]

options.name: --host
options.description:
This option defines the target Beamable environment.
Needed for private cloud customers to target their
exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone
else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --access-token
options.description:
The access token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json
for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --refresh-token
options.description:
A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites
the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS
INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a
./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool,
then any global invocation of the beam tool will
automatically redirect and call the local version.
However, there will be a performance penalty due to
the extra process invocation. This option flag will
cause an error to occur instead of automatically
redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally
running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to
use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be
visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will
attempt to resolve docker through its usual install
locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE
environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

options.name: --emit-log-streams
options.description:
Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to
however they are logged

options.name: --add-project-path
options.description:
additional file paths to be included when building a
local project manifest.

options.name: --raw
options.description:
Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by
default when the command is being piped

options.name: --pretty
options.description:
Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by
default when the command is not piped

options.name: --dotnet-path
options.description: a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]

options.name: --help

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Plan a deployment for later release

options.name: --comment
options.description:
Associates this comment along with the published
Manifest. You'll be able to read it via the
Beamable Portal []

options.name: --service-comments
options.description:
Any number of strings in the format
BeamoId::Comment
Associates each comment to the given Beamo Id if
it's among the published services. You'll be
able to read it via the Beamable Portal []

<from-manifest-id>                               plan

options.name: --run-health-checks
options.description: Run health checks on services

options.name: --redeploy
options.description: Restart existing deployed services

options.name: --build-sequentially
options.description:
Build services sequentially instead of all
together

options.name: --merge
options.description:
Create a Release that merges your current local
environment to the existing remote services.
Existing deployed services will not be removed

options.name: --replace
options.description:
Create a Release that completely overrides the
existing remote services. Existing deployed
services that are not present locally will be
removed (default)

options.name: --docker-compose-dir
options.description:
Specify an output path where a new
docker-compose project will be created. The
compose file can be used to run services
locally. (Note, existing files in this folder
will be overwritten) []

options.name: --sln
options.description:
Relative path to the .sln file to use for the
new project. If the .sln file does not exist, it
will be created. When no option is configured,
if this command is executing inside a .beamable
folder, then the first .sln found in
.beamable/.. will be used. If no .sln is found,
the .sln path will be <name>.sln. If no
.beamable folder exists, then the
<project>/<project>.sln will be used []

options.description: A file path to save the plan

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a
./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool,
then any global invocation of the beam tool will
automatically redirect and call the local version.
However, there will be a performance penalty due to
the extra process invocation. This option flag will
cause an error to occur instead of automatically
redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally
running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to
use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be
visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will
attempt to resolve docker through its usual install
locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE
environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
When true, skip input waiting and use default
arguments (or error if no defaults are possible)
[default: False]

options.name: --host
options.description:
This option defines the target Beamable environment.
Needed for private cloud customers to target their
exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone
else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --access-token
options.description:
The access token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json
for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --refresh-token
options.description:
A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites
the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS
INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a
./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool,
then any global invocation of the beam tool will
automatically redirect and call the local version.
However, there will be a performance penalty due to
the extra process invocation. This option flag will
cause an error to occur instead of automatically
redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally
running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to
use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be
visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will
attempt to resolve docker through its usual install
locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE
environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

options.description:

however they are logged

By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

identified. [default: docker]

redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --pid
options.description:
PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any
Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in
'.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
When true, skip input waiting and use default
arguments (or error if no defaults are possible)
[default: False]

options.name: --host
options.description:
This option defines the target Beamable environment.
Needed for private cloud customers to target their
exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone
else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --access-token
options.description:
The access token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json
for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --refresh-token
options.description:
A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites
the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS
INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a
./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool,
then any global invocation of the beam tool will
automatically redirect and call the local version.
However, there will be a performance penalty due to
the extra process invocation. This option flag will
cause an error to occur instead of automatically
redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally
running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to
use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be
visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will
attempt to resolve docker through its usual install
locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE
environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

options.name: --emit-log-streams
options.description:
Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to
however they are logged

options.name: --add-project-path
options.description:
additional file paths to be included when building a
local project manifest.

options.name: --raw
options.description:
Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by
default when the command is being piped

options.name: --pretty
options.description:
Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by
default when the command is not piped

options.name: --dotnet-path
options.description: a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: NAME
arguments.description: Name of the new project

arguments.name: OUTPUT
arguments.description: Where the temp project will be created []

arguments.name: COPY-PATH
arguments.description: The path to where the files will be copied to []

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Removes the local source code for a service or storage

options.name: --sln
options.description:
Relative path to the .sln file to use for the new
project. If the .sln file does not exist, it will be
created. When no option is configured, if this command
is executing inside a .beamable folder, then the first
.sln found in .beamable/.. will be used. If no .sln is
found, the .sln path will be <name>.sln. If no
.beamable folder exists, then the
<project>/<project>.sln will be used []

options.name: --service-directory
options.description:
Relative path to directory where project should be
created. Defaults to "SOLUTION_DIR/services"

options.name: --ids
options.description:
The list of services to include, defaults to all local
services (separated by whitespace). To use NO
services, use the --exact-ids flag

options.name: --exact-ids
options.description:
By default, a blank --ids option maps to ALL available
ids. When the --exact-ids flag is given, a blank --ids
option maps to NO ids

options.name: --without-groups
options.description:
A set of BeamServiceGroup tags that will exclude the
<without-group>                            associated services. Exclusion takes precedence over
inclusion

options.name: --with-groups
options.description:
A set of BeamServiceGroup tags that will include the
associated services

options.description:

options.description:
[DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as
possible without making any network calls

options.name: --cid
options.description:
CID (CustomerId) to use (found in
Portal->Account); defaults to whatever is in
'.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --engine
options.description:
If passed, sets the engine integration that is
calling for the command

options.name: --engine-sdk-version
options.description:
The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that
Engine

options.name: --engine-version
options.description: The version of the engine that is calling the CLI

options.name: --pid
options.description:
PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games ->
Any Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in
'.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
When true, skip input waiting and use default
arguments (or error if no defaults are possible)
[default: False]

options.name: --host
options.description:
This option defines the target Beamable
environment. Needed for private cloud customers to
target their exclusive Beamable environment.
Ignorable by everyone else. Stored in
'.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --access-token
options.description:
The access token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in
auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --refresh-token
options.description:
A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in
auth.beam.json for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with
a ./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam
tool, then any global invocation of the beam tool
will automatically redirect and call the local
version. However, there will be a performance
penalty due to the extra process invocation. This
option flag will cause an error to occur instead
of automatically redirecting the execution to a
new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer
locally running Microservices. However, if you
need the CLI to use the remotely running
Microservices, use this option to ignore locally
running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will
be visible in their full text. This is a security
risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a
temp file so that they can be used in an error
case. However, when this option is enabled, logs
are not written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG
environment variable is set, no log file will be
written.  [default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI
will attempt to resolve docker through its usual
install locations. You can also use the
BEAM_DOCKER_EXE environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

options.name: --emit-log-streams
options.description:
Out all log messages as data payloads in addition
to however they are logged

options.description:
additional file paths to be included when building
a local project manifest.

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Stop a running service

options.name: --ids
options.description:
The list of services to include, defaults to all local
services (separated by whitespace). To use NO
services, use the --exact-ids flag

options.name: --exact-ids
options.description:
By default, a blank --ids option maps to ALL available
ids. When the --exact-ids flag is given, a blank --ids
option maps to NO ids

options.name: --kill-task
options.description:
Kill the task instead of sending a graceful shutdown
signal via the socket [default: False]

options.name: --reason
options.description:
The reason for stopping the service [default:
cli-request]

options.description:
If it is false the collector process will run
indefinitely [default: True]

options.name: --dryrun
options.description:
[DEPRECATED] Run as much of the command as possible
without making any network calls

options.name: --cid
--engine
options.description:
If passed, sets the engine integration that is calling
for the command

options.name: --engine-sdk-version
options.description:
The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that
Engine

options.name: --engine-version
options.description: The version of the engine that is calling the CLI

options.name: --pid
options.description:
PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any
Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in
'.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
When true, skip input waiting and use default
arguments (or error if no defaults are possible)
[default: False]

options.name: --host
options.description:
This option defines the target Beamable environment.
Needed for private cloud customers to target their
exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone
else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --access-token
options.description:
The access token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json
for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --refresh-token
options.description:
A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites
the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS
INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a
./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool,
then any global invocation of the beam tool will
automatically redirect and call the local version.
However, there will be a performance penalty due to
the extra process invocation. This option flag will
cause an error to occur instead of automatically
redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally
running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to
use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be
visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will
attempt to resolve docker through its usual install
locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE
environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

options.name: --emit-log-streams
options.description:
Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to
however they are logged

options.name: --add-project-path
options.description:
additional file paths to be included when building a
local project manifest.

options.name: --raw
options.description:
Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by
default when the command is being piped

options.name: --pretty
options.description:
Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by
default when the command is not piped

options.name: --dotnet-path
options.description: a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: logs
commands.description: Fetch logs from Clickhouse

options.name: --id
options.description: Filter logs by doing a full match with the service name

options.name: --log-level
options.description:
Filter logs by doing a full match with the Log Level.
Available values are: ["Trace", "Debug",
"Information", "Warning", "Error", "Critical", "None"]

options.name: --limit
options.description:
Sets a max number of rows to be retrieved by this
command [default: 100]

options.name: --full-match
options.description:
If set, this will make the body message match be a
full exact match

options.name: --from
options.description: The amount of time to go back and retrieve logs.

arguments.name: FILTER
arguments.description: Value to be matched with the log message body []

commands.name: prune
commands.description: Clears telemetry data that was exported to files by normal usage of the CLI

options.name: --delete-all
options.description:
If set, will delete all telemetry files [default:
False]

options.name: --retaining-days
options.description:
Can be passed to define a custom amount of days in
which data should be preserved [default: 10]

options.name: --process-id
options.description:
Defines the process Id that called this method. If is
not passed a new process ID will be generated []

commands.name: push
commands.description:
Pushes local telemetry data saved through the BeamableExporter to a running collector. This uses
the Open Telemetry OTLP exporter to push telemetry from files to a running collector using Http
protocol

options.name: --endpoint
options.description:
The endpoint to which the telemetry data should be
exported

options.name: --process-id
options.description:
Defines the process Id that called this method. If is
not passed a new process ID will be generated []

The version of the Beamable's SDK running in that
Engine

options.name: --engine-version
options.description: The version of the engine that is calling the CLI

options.name: --pid
options.description:
PID (Realm ID) to use (found in Portal -> Games -> Any
Realm's details); defaults to whatever is in
'.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
When true, skip input waiting and use default
arguments (or error if no defaults are possible)
[default: False]

options.name: --host
options.description:
This option defines the target Beamable environment.
Needed for private cloud customers to target their
exclusive Beamable environment. Ignorable by everyone
else. Stored in '.beamable/config.beam.json'

options.name: --access-token
options.description:
The access token to use for the requests. It
overwrites the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json
for THIS INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --refresh-token
options.description:
A Refresh Token to use for the requests. It overwrites
the logged in user stored in auth.beam.json for THIS
INVOCATION ONLY

options.name: --logs
options.description: Extra logs gets printed out

options.name: --no-redirect
options.description:
If there is a local dotnet tool installation (with a
./config/dotnet-tools.json file) for the beam tool,
then any global invocation of the beam tool will
automatically redirect and call the local version.
However, there will be a performance penalty due to
the extra process invocation. This option flag will
cause an error to occur instead of automatically
redirecting the execution to a new process invocation.

options.name: --prefer-remote-federation
options.description:
By default, any local CLI invocation that should
trigger a Federation of any type will prefer locally
running Microservices. However, if you need the CLI to
use the remotely running Microservices, use this
option to ignore locally running services.

options.name: --unmask-logs
options.description:
By default, logs will automatically mask tokens.
However, when this option is enabled, tokens will be
visible in their full text. This is a security risk.

options.name: --no-log-file
options.description:
By default, logs are automatically written to a temp
file so that they can be used in an error case.
However, when this option is enabled, logs are not
written. Also, if the BEAM_CLI_NO_FILE_LOG environment
variable is set, no log file will be written.
[default: False]

options.name: --docker-cli-path
options.description:
a custom location for docker. By default, the CLI will
attempt to resolve docker through its usual install
locations. You can also use the BEAM_DOCKER_EXE
environment variable to specify.
Currently, a docker path has been automatically
identified. [default: docker]

options.name: --emit-log-streams
options.description:
Out all log messages as data payloads in addition to
however they are logged

options.name: --add-project-path
options.description:
additional file paths to be included when building a
local project manifest.

options.name: --raw
options.description:
Output raw JSON to standard out. This happens by
default when the command is being piped

options.name: --pretty
options.description:
Output syntax highlighted box text. This happens by
default when the command is not piped

options.name: --dotnet-path
options.description: a custom location for dotnet [default: dotnet]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --older-than
options.description:
Only clear logs older than a given value. This string
should be in a duration format.
The duration format should be a number, followed by a
time unit. Valid time units include seconds (s),
minutes (m), hours (h), days (d), and months(mo).
Please note that the month unit is short-hand for 30
days. Here are a few examples,
